metadata.title: Organizing vs. Deciding: Meeting Notes That Misrepresent

beats.title: Liam cold open — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: Ciao, Liam — in for Bear. This is Liam, in for Bear? Can you explain it, Bear?

props.command: This is Liam, in for Bear?
props.segment: Organizing vs. Deciding: Meeting Notes That

beats.narration_text: She asked Cowork to summarize Tuesday's strategy meeting notes with action items. The summary arrived — polished, organized, every action item named with an owner. Three people pushed back the next day. Two action items had been assigned to team members who had not been in the room for the discussion that produced them. One item marked 'decided' had been tabled. The notes sounded authoritative. They had not recorded what was decided. They had decided what was recorded. This is Liam, in for Bear.
beats.new_visual_element: ClaudeComposerAsk — cold open, summarize strategy meeting notes with action items

props.command: Summarize Tuesday's strategy meeting notes with action items

beats.narration_text: Four operations ranked by risk: organizing — chronological structure, no interpretation, lowest risk. Summarizing — compression with selection, moderate risk. Synthesizing — integrating across multiple speakers, higher risk. Deciding — resolving discussion into commitment, the human's job. Cowork performs all four fluently. The output does not announce which operation is happening. The brief must restrict the task to the operation you authorized.
beats.new_visual_element: Four operations — organizing / summarizing / synthesizing / deciding / risk increases / brief must restrict

beats.narration_text: The evidence-in-notes column is the structural fix. For every action item in the output, Cowork must cite the exact phrase from the source notes that produced it. 'Exact phrase from source required for every item' is not a style preference — it forces Cowork to anchor synthesis to evidence, not to create plausible continuations of the discussion. If the phrase does not exist in the notes, the item cannot appear as decided.
beats.new_visual_element: Evidence in notes — exact phrase required / anchors synthesis to source / no phrase = not decided / column in table
beats.spark_line: Exact phrase from source. Every item.

beats.narration_text: Two rules for items without a clear owner or status: owner not stated goes to Unresolved, not assigned. Tabled items go to Tabled with the tabled-by phrase, not Decided. 'Mark uncertain — do not infer' is the instruction. Cowork's default is coherence. Your brief must override that default with a specific instruction to leave gaps visible rather than filling them with a plausible assignment.
beats.new_visual_element: Mark uncertain — no owner → Unresolved / tabled → Tabled / do not infer / override coherence default

beats.narration_text: The human-confirmed column is filled by you after reading the original notes — not by Cowork. Cowork outputs a draft. You read the source. You mark which items you can confirm from your direct knowledge of the meeting. Items you cannot confirm go back to Unresolved until a participant confirms them. That column is the gate between Cowork's synthesis and an authoritative record.
beats.new_visual_element: Artifact view — human-confirmed column / filled by you / unconfirmed → Unresolved / gate between synthesis and record

beats.narration_text: Add the evidence and human-confirmed columns before your next meeting summary. Paste this.
beats.new_visual_element: HANDOFF — meeting notes brief with evidence and confirmed columns

props.command: Summarize these meeting notes as a table with four columns: (1) Item — the action or decision, (2) Owner — name only if stated in the notes; otherwise write Unresolved, (3) Evidence — the exact phrase from the source notes that produced this item, (4) Human Confirmed — leave blank (I will fill this after reviewing the originals). Do not infer owners. Do not mark items Decided unless the exact phrase appears. Tabled items get status Tabled with the tabling phrase. [paste notes]

beats.title: Liam verdict — ClaudeVerdictArtifact
beats.narration_text: Here's what the body just demonstrated. The human-confirmed column is filled by you after reading the original notes — not by Cowork. Cowork outputs a draft. You read the source. You mark which items you can confirm from your direct knowledge of the meeting. Items you cannot confirm go back to Unresolved until a participant confirms them. That column is the gate between Cowork's synthesis and an authoritative record. Add the evidence and human-confirmed columns before your next meeting summary. Paste this.

beats.title: Your turn handoff — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: Take this prompt, run it on your own — pick any cancer type or clinical scenario you know about and ask how this mechanism applies there.

props.command: Explain how [Organizing vs. Deciding: Meeting Notes That Misrepresent] applies to a specific cancer type or clinical case you're studying. What proteins are involved, what goes wrong at the molecular level, and what is the therapeutic or diagnostic significance?
